title: onobrychis subacaulis
intro: Onobrychis subacaulis, also known as the stemless bird's foot trefoil, is a perennial herb belonging to the legume family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exerting antimicrobial properties. Onobrychis subacaulis is typically prepared as a herbal infusion or decoction, and its extracts have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
